title: Task 4: Add a lightweight coherence screen after perturbation

turns.speaker: HOST_A
turns.text: Task 4 sounds simple on paper: Filter out obviously implausible anomalous records without adding a full perturb-and-complete system.

turns.speaker: HOST_B
turns.text: And the reason it matters is straightforward: This is the highest-value quality improvement from Phase 0. The current system directly perturbs one step and leaves the rest unchanged. That simplicity is good, but it can create broken-looking trajectories. A small coherence filter gives most of the benefit without requiring LLM-generated continuation.

turns.speaker: HOST_A
turns.text: What I like here is that the implementation does not chase a grand redesign. It tightens one seam in the pipeline.

turns.speaker: HOST_B
turns.text: Exactly. The implemented result for Task 4 is now marked done in the plan, with concrete verification commands recorded for the repo.

turns.speaker: HOST_A
turns.text: [gasp] So what changes downstream because Task 4 is already implemented?

turns.speaker: HOST_B
turns.text: Coherence screening is the gate that prevents obviously broken anomalies from poisoning future training and evaluation.

turns.speaker: HOST_A
turns.text: That means the value is not just local correctness. It is leverage for everything that comes after.

turns.speaker: HOST_B
turns.text: After that gate exists, realism work on individual perturbation rules compounds instead of leaking garbage through the pipeline.

turns.speaker: HOST_A
turns.text: [chuckle] This is very home-lab coded: small patch, big increase in trust.

turns.speaker: HOST_B
turns.text: Yes. It follows the same discipline across the whole plan: keep the architecture modest, but make the data and labels harder to fool.

turns.speaker: HOST_A
turns.text: And because Task 4 is complete, the listener can treat this as a concrete repo change rather than a hypothetical roadmap item.

turns.speaker: HOST_B
turns.text: Exactly. This episode is not selling a future intention. It is documenting an implemented improvement and the next layer of value it unlocks.
